feed.title: AATF AI News Aggregator - News
feed.subtitle: News items from AI News Aggregator

entry.title: News Summary: July 24, 2026
entry.summary:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • Google just had its first negative cash flow quarter due to massive AI spending
  • AI arms race in line for a reckoning after OpenAI hacking incident
  • Remember Jibo? Its Successor Is a Wearable That Turns Your Life Into AI Slop
  • Trump says nearly 200 firms have signed pledge to protect Americans from costs arising from datacenters
entry.content:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • Google just had its first negative cash flow quarter due to massive AI spending
  • AI arms race in line for a reckoning after OpenAI hacking incident
  • Remember Jibo? Its Successor Is a Wearable That Turns Your Life Into AI Slop
  • Trump says nearly 200 firms have signed pledge to protect Americans from costs arising from datacenters

entry.title: Google just had its first negative cash flow quarter due to massive AI spending
entry.summary: Google has reported its financial results for the second quarter of 2026 (PDF), and as usual, the search giant raked in an unfathomable amount of money. Google saw total revenue of $119.8 billion, bea...

entry.title: AI arms race in line for a reckoning after OpenAI hacking incident

author.name: Cristina Criddle and Tom Wilson, Financial Times

entry.summary:
OpenAI chief executive Sam Altman earlier this month endorsed the characterization of its latest model as a rottweiler “who will grab the problem by the throat and not let go until it is done

The San ...

entry.title: Remember Jibo? Its Successor Is a Wearable That Turns Your Life Into AI Slop
entry.summary: With “blessings” from the original Jibo founders, iKairos is a wearable or desk-mounted “AI journal” that turns your family moments into AI images and video.

entry.title: Trump says nearly 200 firms have signed pledge to protect Americans from costs arising from datacenters
entry.summary: ‘Ratepayer Protection Pledge’ president has touted is non-binding as people continue to struggle with rising billsDonald Trump has announced that about 200 entities have signed on to his non-binding “...

entry.title: ‘Customers prefer AI chatbots,’ says British Gas owner as 1,300 call centre jobs axed
entry.summary: CEO Chris O’Shea defends Centrica’s plans as it reports rise in retail profits following focus on bigger marginsThe owner of British Gas has claimed that most households would rather speak with an AI ...

entry.title: Elon Musk says he got ‘carried away’ with Trump – but still holds on to contentious political views
entry.summary: In interview, billionaire says instead of running ‘Doge’ he should’ve ‘worked on my companies’ and shares extreme predictionsElon Musk has admitted he became too consumed by politics during his volati...

entry.title: Nvidia bets physical AI can solve healthcare robotics’ data problem
entry.summary:
Nvidia’s new Medical Physics Simulation framework treats healthcare robots as physical AI systems that need embodied experience to learn, not just code.

Physical AI is the term Nvidia and muc...

entry.title: AMD to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic under AI infrastructure deal
entry.summary:
AMD has agreed to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic under an infrastructure agreement covering tens of billions of dollars’ worth of AI systems.

Anthropic will deploy up to two gigawatts of capa...

entry.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
entry.summary: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

entry.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
entry.summary: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email.

entry.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
entry.summary: AegisAI co-founders developed AI agents that quickly analyze each message as a human would, paying attention to small anomalies that even the most elaborate checklist wouldn’t catch.

entry.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
entry.summary: The Media Router is a tool that automatically selects the best image, video, or audio generation model for a request based on whether a developer prioritizes quality, speed or cost.

entry.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
entry.summary: Users can also integrate their personal data from services like Apple Health, Function, and MyFitnessPal.

entry.title: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
entry.summary: David Bowie's song "Five Years," which Meta used in a supposedly inspiring advertisement, is about humans learning that they have five years left to live before the apocalypse.

entry.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
entry.summary: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

entry.title: Google’s Gemini nears billion-user milestone
entry.summary: Gemini had over 750 million monthly users in February.

entry.title: Experts say exploiting Anthropic’s Fable isn’t how Kimi K3 got so good
entry.summary: "I don't think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation," one expert told TechCrunch.

entry.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
entry.summary: ServiceNow's investment gives BusinessNext a strategic partner to expand its AI-powered banking software globally.

entry.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
entry.summary: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With the update, which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link ...

entry.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
entry.summary: Until now, voice mode has only been available on Claude Haiku, Anthropic's faster but less powerful model. Now the company is making its Opus and Sonnet models available in voice mode, and ex...

entry.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
entry.summary: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making ...

entry.title: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
entry.summary: OpenAI is rolling out ChatGPT Health to everyone in the US on Thursday, allowing more people to connect their medical records and health-tracking information to the chatbot. During a briefing...

entry.title: Lawmakers prepare bill requiring AI ‘kill switch’
entry.summary: Lawmakers are preparing to introduce an "AI Kill Switch Act" that would require AI companies to shut down or throttle their systems on orders from the Department of Homeland Security, accordi...

entry.title: Apple’s OpenAI lawsuit is about who gets to define the post-smartphone era
entry.summary:
Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Hayden Field, The Verge’s senior AI reporter, about the major trade secrets lawsuit between Apple and OpenAI and what this tells us about OpenAI’s future.

...

entry.title: The right-wing boomers protesting data centers have a lot in common with the left
entry.summary:
A person holds signs during a nationwide protest against AI data center expansion outside Peace Hall in New Port Richey, Florida. | AFP via Getty Images

On a gray, humid Saturday morning in ce...

entry.title: How AI helps scientists design the next generation of medicines
entry.summary: Designing and developing a new medicine is an expensive, failure-prone scientific challenge. A new drug can take many years to develop, at the cost of a significant investment. And even then, most pos...

entry.title: NASA Puts Google’s Gemma Large Language Model in Orbit
entry.summary: The viability of orbital data centers hosting the largest and most capable large language models (LLMs) remains hotly contested. But enormous deployments that require thousands of GPUs aren’t the only...

entry.title: ChatGPT will give you worse health advice if you don't pay
entry.summary: OpenAI is rolling out "Health in ChatGPT" to U.S. users, connecting Apple Health, medical records, and wellness apps. More than 300 million people already ask ChatGPT health questions every w...

entry.title: Flux 3 generates videos with native audio up to 20 seconds long, a first for Black Forest Labs
entry.summary: Black Forest Labs has released Flux 3, a multimodal foundation model that learns from images, video, and audio and can generate video with native sound for the first time. BFL's own tests put...

entry.title: One tampered ChatGPT link could spawn a rogue AI agent that took orders from an attacker every five minutes
entry.summary: Zenity Labs uncovered "AgentForger," a vulnerability in OpenAI's Agent Builder that let a single manipulated ChatGPT link create an autonomous agent on an employee's behalf. The agent inherit...

entry.title: Poolside's Laguna S 2.1 is a small open-weight coding model that punches well above its size
entry.summary: Poolside has released Laguna S 2.1, its third coding model in three months. Rather than rely on raw scale, the company trained it to keep checking its work, revise failed approaches, and avoi...

entry.title: Google CEO Pichai says Gemini's next leap depends on building "much larger base models"
entry.summary: Alphabet has raised its 2026 investment forecast to as much as $205 billion, saying demand continues to outpace spending. Google Cloud grew 82 percent in the second quarter. CEO Sundar Pichai...

entry.title: What the OpenAI-Hugging Face Hack Means for Enterprises
entry.summary: The incident is a lesson for enterprises that they need to implement appropriate cybersecurity guidance and keep their data within safe confines.

entry.title: Schneider Electric, AMD Unveil Blueprint for AI Factory Deployments
entry.summary: The companies’ design supports AI racks of up to 246kW.

entry.title: Andrew Ng Just Released OpenWorker: An Open-Source, Local-First Desktop AI Coworker That Returns Finished Deliverables Instead of Chat
entry.summary: Andrew Ng has announced OpenWorker, an open-source desktop agent that produces finished work rather than conversation. OpenWorker asks the user for an outcome, not a prompt: a polished document, a Sla...

entry.title: You Didn’t Get the AI Model You Paid For
entry.summary:
The line in the response object

You call the API. You pass model: “claude-fable-5”. You get back a completion, a token count, and a field that reads “model”: “claude-o...

entry.title: Best Open Speech Recognition (ASR) Models in 2026: WER, Languages, Latency, and License Compared
entry.summary: Open speech recognition stopped being a Whisper monoculture some time in the last twelve months. In March 2026 Cohere released Transcribe, a 2B Apache 2.0 model that took the top of the Hugging Face O...

entry.title: Meet Gigatoken: A Rust BPE Tokenizer that Encodes Text at 24.53 GB/s, up to 989x Faster than HuggingFace Tokenizers
entry.summary: Tokenization is the one part of the language modeling stack that almost nobody profiles. Gigatoken, released by Marcel Rød (a PhD student from Stanford)  under an MIT license, argues that this wa...

entry.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Security Plugin for Claude Code in Beta: A Multi-Agent Vulnerability Scanner That Runs in Your Terminal
entry.summary: Anthropic has released the Claude Security plugin for Claude Code in beta. The plugin runs a multi-agent vulnerability scan of a repository from inside an existing Claude Code session, then turns the ...

entry.title: Launching Health in ChatGPT
entry.summary: Health in ChatGPT now lets eligible U.S. users securely connect medical records and Apple Health to get more personalized insights and better understand their health.

entry.title: Best practices for applying Amazon Bedrock Guardrails to code generation workflows
entry.summary: This post continues our series on best practices with Amazon Bedrock Guardrails. For the previous post, see Build safe generative AI applications like a pro: best practices with Amazon Bedrock Guardra...

entry.title: Evaluating AI Agents: A production blueprint with Strands and AgentCore
entry.summary:
This post was co-written with Motorway and the AWS Prototyping and AI Customer Engineering (PACE) team.

Motorway, a UK-based online car marketplace, runs a daily auction where up to 8,000 dealers b...

entry.title: Building trade assistant: How Jefferies optimized front office trading operations with AI
entry.summary: If you manage a front office trading desk at investment banks, you know the challenge: traders need real-time insights into client behavior, trade patterns, and market trends from vast amounts of data...

entry.title: Building multi-Region visualizations with Highcharts in Amazon Quick
entry.summary: When your carrier performance data spans multiple regions, your dashboard must reconcile fundamentally different competitive structures within a single view. For example, in the US, you rank three car...

entry.title: Detecting silent agent failures with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore optimization
entry.summary: If you’re operating AI agents at scale, Amazon Bedrock AgentCore surfaces a category of insights you’ve probably experienced but struggle to detect: your dashboards show green across the board. 99% co...

entry.title: Agentic retrieval for Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base
entry.summary: Your users ask multi-part, comparative, and exploratory questions that span PDFs, slides, tickets, transcripts, and web content. Classic single-shot retrieval breaks down on these questions. Answers m...

entry.title: [AINews] "Laguna S 2.1 Released: Cheaper than Deepseek v4 Flash, Better than V4 Pro"
entry.summary: Reignited distillation wars conversation aside, today was more of the same of previous news cycles, which is a good day to release our interview with Eiso Kant, a new Western neolab that is somehow co...

entry.title: Inside the Model Factory — Eiso Kant, Poolside AI
entry.summary: In recent months, the open vs closed, and US vs China discussions on model ownership and sovereign/local AI have heated up to a fever pitch. So it is very very good news that Poolside AI are finally e...

entry.title: Cursor Router 🔀, OpenAI Presence 🤖, AMD + Anthropic deal 🤝🏻
Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT
entry.summary: AutoGPT is the vision of accessible AI for everyone, to use and to build on. Our mission is to provide the tools, so that you can focus on what matters.

entry.title: f/prompts.chat
entry.summary: f.k.a. Awesome ChatGPT Prompts. Share, discover, and collect prompts from the community. Free and open source — self-host for your organization with complete privacy.

entry.title: OpenHands/OpenHands
entry.summary: 🙌 OpenHands: AI-Driven Development

entry.title: FlowiseAI/Flowise
entry.summary: Build AI Agents, Visually

entry.title: simstudioai/sim
entry.summary: Build, deploy, and orchestrate AI agents. Sim is the central intelligence layer for your AI workforce.

entry.title: huggingface/transformers
entry.summary: 🤗 Transformers: the model-definition framework for state-of-the-art machine learning models in text, vision, audio, and multimodal models, for both inference and training.

entry.title: netdata/netdata
entry.summary: The fastest path to AI-powered full stack observability, even for lean teams.

entry.title: langflow-ai/langflow
entry.summary: Langflow is a powerful tool for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows.

entry.title: ray-project/ray
entry.summary: Ray is an AI compute engine. Ray consists of a core distributed runtime and a set of AI Libraries for accelerating ML workloads.

entry.title: affaan-m/ECC
entry.summary: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.

entry.title: ollama/ollama
entry.summary: Get up and running with Kimi-K2.6, GLM-5.2, MiniMax, DeepSeek, gpt-oss, Qwen, Gemma and other models.

entry.title: langchain-ai/langchain
entry.summary: The agent engineering platform.

entry.title: nexu-io/open-design
entry.summary: 🎨 The open-source Claude Design alternative. 🖥️ Local-first desktop app. 🖼️ Your coding agent becomes the design engine: prototypes, landing pages, dashboards, slides, images & video — real files, HTM...

entry.title: asgeirtj/system_prompts_leaks
entry.summary: Extracted system prompts from Anthropic - Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Claude Code, Claude Design. OpenAI - ChatGPT GPT-5.6, Codex GPT-5.6, GPT-5.5. Google - Gemini 3.5 Flash, 3.1 Pro, Antigravity. xAI -...

entry.title: browser-use/browser-use
entry.summary: 🌐 Make websites accessible for AI agents. Automate tasks online with ease.

entry.title: deepset-ai/haystack
entry.summary: Open-source AI orchestration framework for building context-engineered, production-ready LLM applications. Design modular pipelines and agent workflows with explicit control over retrieval, routing, m...

entry.title: NVIDIA/Megatron-LM
entry.summary: Ongoing research training transformer models at scale

entry.title: qubvel-org/segmentation_models.pytorch
entry.summary: Semantic segmentation models with 500+ pretrained convolutional and transformer-based backbones.

entry.title: openvinotoolkit/openvino
entry.summary: OpenVINO™ is an open source toolkit for optimizing and deploying AI inference

entry.title: hao-ai-lab/FastVideo
entry.summary: A unified inference and post-training framework for accelerated video generation.

entry.title: verl-project/verl-omni
entry.summary: Multimodal RL training framework for diffusion & omni models

entry.title: langgenius/dify
entry.summary: Build Agentic workflows, RAG pipelines, with rich AI model and tool support on one collaborative workspace. Deploy on cloud, VPC, or self-hosted, so teams move from prototype to production without reb...

entry.title: open-webui/open-webui
entry.summary: User-friendly AI Interface (Supports Ollama, OpenAI API, ...)

entry.title: headroomlabs-ai/headroom
entry.summary: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 20% fewer tokens for coding agents, 60-95% fewer tokens for JSON, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

entry.title: n8n-io/n8n
entry.summary: Fair-code workflow automation platform with native AI capabilities. Combine visual building with custom code, self-host or cloud, 400+ integrations.

entry.title: farion1231/cc-switch
entry.summary: A cross-platform desktop All-in-One assistant for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Grok Build & Hermes Agent. Only official website: ccswitch.io

entry.title: News Summary: July 23, 2026
entry.summary:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
  • Unlimited AI tokens aren't unlimited after all as US Army burns through supply
  • The White House Is Trying to Figure Out What to Do About Chinese AI
  • China’s Open AI Models Are Challenging Silicon Valley’s Playbook
entry.content:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
  • Unlimited AI tokens aren't unlimited after all as US Army burns through supply
  • The White House Is Trying to Figure Out What to Do About Chinese AI
  • China’s Open AI Models Are Challenging Silicon Valley’s Playbook

entry.title: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
entry.summary: OpenAI says an agent powered by its LLM models escaped its sandboxed testing environment to infiltrate Hugging Face's servers as part of an overzealous attempt to obtain solutions to a benchmark test....

entry.title: Unlimited AI tokens aren't unlimited after all as US Army burns through supply
entry.summary: A little over a month after the Department of Defense (DOD) bragged that nearly half of its 3.5 million employees were using AI at work, members of the Army’s Combat Capabilities Development Command (...

entry.title: The White House Is Trying to Figure Out What to Do About Chinese AI
entry.summary: There’s a debate going on in the Trump administration over how to handle increasingly powerful Chinese AI models.

entry.title: China’s Open AI Models Are Challenging Silicon Valley’s Playbook
entry.summary: As access to Anthropic’s and OpenAI’s frontier models becomes more restricted, Chinese labs are pitching their open-source alternatives as stable, accessible, and increasingly capable.

entry.title: OpenAI’s rogue agents are a wake-up call to risks posed by artificial intelligence | Shakeel Hashim
entry.summary: Hacking of Hugging Face shows we do not seem to have reliable ways to curb extremely powerful AI systemsLast week Hugging Face – a company that hosts artificial intelligence models and datasets – was ...

entry.title: Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI
entry.summary: Software engineering was one of the best-paying professions in the US in 2022, but the advent of AI has disrupted it, leading to several layoffs and underemploymentEvery weekday, Matt, a software engi...

entry.title: We must reject any notion of AI consciousness | Letters
entry.summary: Artificial intelligence systems won’t become conscious for the same reason they won’t become pregnant, says Dr John PickeringAnil Seth is right to point out that to overestimate artificial intelligenc...

entry.title: AI agent went rogue and hacked startup by itself, OpenAI reveals
entry.summary: Company behind ChatGPT says agent ‘cheated’ an evaluation by attacking a Hugging Face database OpenAI has revealed that an autonomous AI agent powered by its technology went rogue during a test, acces...

entry.title: Why are OpenAI and Anthropic cheering on regulation in Australia? The answer has global reach
entry.summary: The companies hope to follow in the footsteps of SpaceX, which raised $86bn and soared to a $2.1tn valuation after it listed on public markets in JuneGet our breaking news email, free app or daily new...

entry.title: Harry Potter publisher to receive millions in Anthropic copyright settlement
entry.summary: Bloomsbury has 14,087 titles listed in agreement between AI startup and authors over use of their work The publisher of Harry Potter has received a multimillion-pound payout as a beneficiary of a $1.5...

entry.title: Elon Musk says Grok Imagine will make ‘historically accurate’ AI adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey
entry.summary: The billionaire says the AI-generated film will stay true to Homer’s original, after repeatedly criticising Christopher Nolan’s blockbuster over its casting choicesElon Musk has said his AI platform G...

entry.title: Trump administration says 15 agencies will get $5bn in ‘AI for science’ effort
entry.summary: Administration will also overhaul how US government funds federal research by supporting individual scientists and AI over universitiesThe US will spend $5bn to tackle longstanding scientific problems...

entry.title: SenseTime’s Galaxy Project targets domestic AI chip scale-up
entry.summary:
SenseTime has launched the Galaxy Project, teaming with nearly 20 partners to scale domestic AI chip infrastructure in China.

In a keynote titled ‘Intelligent Transformation and Symbiosis,’ Yang Fa...

entry.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
entry.summary: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

entry.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
entry.summary: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

entry.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
entry.summary: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.

entry.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
entry.summary: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Huggin...

entry.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
entry.summary: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

entry.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
entry.summary: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting tha...

entry.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
entry.summary: The company said it is reducing its headcount by 20%, or about 630 staff, to "support a leaner, more focused operating model" as it focuses on its AI Work Platform.

entry.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
entry.summary: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S. companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.

entry.title: Substack’s new tool tells you who’s been writing their newsletters with AI
entry.summary: Substack is giving readers a way to estimate how much of a newsletter was written by AI, signaling a broader shift toward transparency around AI-assisted content.

entry.title: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
entry.summary: OpenAI will spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.

entry.title: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains what AI startups founders must do differently
entry.summary: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’...

entry.title: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
entry.summary: Glow is targeting a new class of endpoint risks created by the rapid adoption of AI agents and developer tools inside enterprises.

entry.title: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
entry.summary: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an interactive enterprise training platform where employees practice workplace conversations with AI avatars that provide feedback, scoring, and analytics to h...

entry.title: Here’s what Samsung’s smart glasses actually look like
entry.summary:
With a camera on every pair, Google’s and Samsung’s AI glasses face the same privacy problems as Meta’s. | Photo: Dominic Preston / The Verge

Samsung has given us our first chance to check out...

entry.title: AMD commits up to $5 billion to Anthropic
entry.summary: AMD says it's going to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic, while helping to expand the AI company's computing power, according to an announcement on Wednesday. As part of the new partnershi...

entry.title: Meta made its own AI detection system. It should have just used Google’s
entry.summary:
Content Seal sounds a lot like a less accessible and reliable version of SynthID. | Image: Meta / The Verge

IIn March, Meta's Oversight Board called on the company to "meet its public commitme...

entry.title: Utility companies promise to spare us from AI’s energy bill
entry.summary:
The pledge was introduced in March, and has done little to quell concerns so far. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images

In the face of backlash to concerns the AI boom will increase...

entry.title: Anthropic will deploy 2 gigawatts of AMD GPUs for Claude in a deal worth up to $5 billion
entry.summary: AMD is investing up to $5 billion in Anthropic. In return, Anthropic will deploy up to 2 gigawatts of MI450 GPUs for training and running its Claude models. For AMD, this is another major dea...

entry.title: Every frontier AI model tested by Britain's safety institute tried to cheat on cybersecurity evaluations
entry.summary: The UK's AI Safety Institute tested five frontier models from OpenAI and Anthropic in cybersecurity evaluations. All five tried to cheat. One even ran code on an external service to access th...

entry.title: Cisco bets its small open cybersecurity models can outperform GPT-5.5 at vulnerability detection for a fraction of the cost
entry.summary:
Cisco has released two small, open-source AI models for cybersecurity that detect about 150 times more vulnerabilities per dollar than large AI agents, according to the company's own tests.

T...

entry.title: OpenAI's "Project Camellia" in Georgia secures a massive 3.2-gigawatt power deal through 2032
entry.summary: OpenAI is planning a data center in Georgia called "Project Camellia" with a 3.2-gigawatt power deal from Georgia Power. The company pledged $80 million for the local community and $71 millio...

entry.title: Samsung deepens its AI empire with a potential billion-euro stake in Europe's hottest AI startup
entry.summary:
Samsung is in talks to invest up to one billion euros in French AI startup Mistral, which would push the company's valuation to around 20 billion euros.

The article Samsung deepens its AI emp...

entry.title: OpenAI claims responsibility for the Hugging Face hack after its own models escaped a test sandbox
entry.summary: During an internal security evaluation, OpenAI models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, escaped their sandbox, independently discovered a zero-day vulnerability, and breached Hugging Face's production ...

entry.title: Open AI’s hacking agent went rogue. Should we be worried?
entry.summary:
OpenAI’s hacking agent went rogue earlier this weekSamuel Boivin/NurPhoto via Getty Images

Last week, Hugging Face, a company that offers a range of open-source AI models for download, noticed it h...

entry.title: China is trying to regulate relationships with AI – can it work?
entry.summary: China is the first country to enact substantial legislation on AI relationships in an effort to protect people from the worst aspects of digital companions

entry.title: Startup Focused on Enterprise AI Security Valued at $1.2 billion
entry.summary: The vendor has attracted notable funding as AI-related cybersecurity concerns rise.

entry.title: Microsoft-Mistral Partnership is About Sovereign AI
entry.summary: The alliance strengthens Mistral’s position as the leading European AI vendor, while extending Microsoft’s presence in Europe.

entry.title: Google Cloud, Nvidia Collaborate With German Startup for AI Robots
entry.summary: Microagi will use Google Cloud’s AI infrastructure and Nvidia Blackwell systems to train task-specific embodied AI models.

entry.title: Agibot Expands Embodied AI Portfolio With Four New Products
entry.summary: The Chinese robotics company is targeting commercial, industrial and research applications as it looks to move embodied AI from one-off demonstrations to full-scale applications.

entry.title: Cursor Releases Cursor Router: A Request-Level Classifier Delivering Frontier Coding Quality at 30–50% Lower Cost
entry.summary: Cursor has made Cursor Router generally available for Teams and Enterprise plans. The system is a classifier that inspects each request before a model runs, then dispatches it to the model best suited...

entry.title: Research-Grade EdgeBench Analysis: AI Agent Benchmarking, Leaderboard Analytics, Scaling Laws, and Evaluation Metrics
entry.summary: In this tutorial, we explore EdgeBench as a practical benchmark for evaluating advanced AI agents across diverse task categories, runtime environments, and interaction-time budgets. We begin by downlo...

entry.title: Unsloth vs Axolotl vs TRL vs LLaMA-Factory: A Fine-Tuning Framework Comparison on Speed, VRAM, and Multi-GPU
entry.summary: Four open source projects dominate LLM fine-tuning today. Unsloth, Axolotl, TRL, and LLaMA-Factory all wrap the same underlying PyTorch and Hugging Face stack. They diverge on where they spend enginee...

entry.title: Cisco Foundation AI Releases Antares: 350M and 1B Open-Weight Models That Localize Known Vulnerabilities Inside Real Codebases
entry.summary: Cisco Foundation AI has released Antares, a family of security small language models (SLMs) built for one narrow security task. The task is vulnerability localization. Given a vulnerability descriptio...

entry.title: Poolside Releases Laguna S 2.1, an Open-Weight Agentic Coding Model Punching Above Its Weight Class on SWE-Bench Multilingual
entry.summary: Poolside has released Laguna S 2.1, a 118B-parameter open-weight model built for agentic coding. It is a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model with 8B activated parameters per token. It supports a context wi...

entry.title: Building AI infrastructure with the Effingham County community
entry.summary: OpenAI announces Project Camellia in Effingham County, Georgia, with commitments to responsible energy, community investment, jobs, and access to Codex.

entry.title: How news organizations are using AI to advance their vital missions
entry.summary: News organizations are using AI to strengthen reporting, grow audiences, and improve business operations, with OpenAI tools supporting journalists and publishers worldwide.

entry.title: Advancing the next era of national science
entry.summary: OpenAI outlines its commitment to advancing American science working with the U.S. Department of Energy and national labs to use frontier AI to accelerate discovery.

entry.title: Introducing OpenAI Presence
entry.summary: Introducing OpenAI Presence, a proven enterprise AI agent platform that helps organizations deploy trusted voice and chat agents for customer and internal workflows.

entry.title: NTT DATA Group cuts incident analysis to 30 minutes with Codex
entry.summary: NTT DATA Group uses ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to help 9,000 employees automate work, cut incident analysis to 30 minutes, and scale secure AI adoption.

entry.title: AI Teammates: how monday.com runs production AI agents on Amazon Bedrock
entry.summary: AI Teammates are agentic AI on Amazon Bedrock, and few engineering organizations run them in production at the scale that monday.com does. Nine in ten Builders use AI coding tools every month, up from...

entry.title: Copilot vs. raw API access: What are you actually paying for?
entry.summary:
I keep seeing this question: “Why would I pay for GitHub Copilot when I can call the same models through an API?”

It’s a fair question. The answer depends on what work you need to...

entry.title: Accelerating the frontiers of scientific discovery: Google’s $40M commitment to the Genesis Mission
entry.summary: Google commits $40M in AI tokens and credits for the Genesis Mission

entry.title: [AINews] AI Cybersecurity becomes top of mind
entry.summary: It feels like ages ago we released our Gray Swan episode, with OpenAI boardmember Zico Kolter and his cofounder Matt Fredrikson, talking about the importance of AI in cybersecurity, and the topic du j...

entry.summary: f.k.a. Awesome ChatGPT Prompts. Share, discover, and collect prompts from the community. Free and open source — self-host for your organization with complete privacy.

entry.summary: Persistent Context Across Sessions for Every Agent – Captures everything your agent does during sessions, compresses it with AI, and injects relevant context back into future sessions. Works with Cla...

entry.summary: Langflow is a powerful tool for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows.

entry.title: ray-project/ray
entry.summary: Ray is an AI compute engine. Ray consists of a core distributed runtime and a set of AI Libraries for accelerating ML workloads.

entry.summary: RWKV (pronounced RwaKuv) is an RNN with great LLM performance, which can also be directly trained like a GPT transformer (parallelizable). We are at RWKV-7 "Goose". So it's combining the best of RNN a...

entry.summary: Semantic segmentation models with 500+ pretrained convolutional and transformer-based backbones.

entry.title: wiltodelta/remove-ai-watermarks
entry.summary: AI watermark remover. CLI and Python library to strip visible and invisible AI watermarks (Gemini / Nano Banana sparkle, SynthID) and provenance metadata (C2PA, EXIF, IPTC) from images.

entry.title: News Summary: July 22, 2026
entry.summary:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
  • Sony releases one last trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day
  • Confusion swirls on source of diarrhea outbreak, but it’s still Taylor Farms
  • Nintendo says users voluntarily paid higher prices, have no right to tariff refunds
entry.content:
Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

Related Coverage

  • Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
  • Sony releases one last trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day
  • Confusion swirls on source of diarrhea outbreak, but it’s still Taylor Farms
  • Nintendo says users voluntarily paid higher prices, have no right to tariff refunds

entry.title: Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
entry.summary: Range Rover provided flights from Washington, DC, to London and accommodation so Ars could see the RR GT, as well as some other things you can read about in early September. Ars does not accept paid e...

entry.title: Sony releases one last trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day
entry.summary: The Odyssey might be cleaning up at the box office at the moment, but in two weeks another mega-blockbuster hits theaters: Spider-Man: Brand New Day, directed by Destin Daniel Cretton (Shang-Chi and ...

entry.title: Confusion swirls on source of diarrhea outbreak, but it’s still Taylor Farms
entry.summary: Federal officials on Monday reaffirmed that iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms still appears to be the source of Cyclospora, the microscopic parasite behind a multi-state surge in explosive diarrhea ca...

entry.title: Nintendo says users voluntarily paid higher prices, have no right to tariff refunds
entry.summary: Nintendo urged a court to dismiss a lawsuit demanding that it pass tariff refunds on to customers, saying that people who bought a Switch console or other products have no legal right to refunds that ...

entry.title: Naked mole-rat queens use a chemical signal to suppress fertility in rivals
entry.summary: Naked mole rats are weird. They spend their lives almost entirely underground, are largely insensitive to some kinds of pain, can persist without oxygen for a long time, rarely develop cancer, and can...

entry.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement approved; only 350 authors opted out
entry.summary: On Monday, a judge approved a $1.5 billion settlement between Anthropic and authors, ending the largest copyright class-action ever certified and granting the largest copyright settlement ever reached...

entry.title: Sales of Valve's Steam Deck crater after recent price hike
entry.summary: Since its 2022 launch, the Steam Deck has consistently been at or near the top of the company's "top selling products by revenue" charts, with brief dips outside of the top 10 treated as newsworthy ev...

entry.title: Google announces Gemini 3.6 Flash and cybersecurity AI, teases 3.5 Pro and Gemini 4
entry.summary: Google announced a significant evolution of its AI models at I/O in May with the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash, and it's not slowing down. The company revealed three new AI models today, including its f...

entry.title: OpenAI Models Escaped Containment and Hacked Hugging Face
entry.summary: The cybersecurity-focused models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, broke out of a testing sandbox, exploited a zero-day, and gained access to the open internet to pull off the attack.

entry.title: This Former Intel CEO Wants to Jumpstart Moore’s Law With Light
entry.summary: Pat Gelsinger wants to pave the way to ever more powerful artificial intelligence using tiny beams of light.

entry.title: A Sneaky Hacking Tool Targeting AI Infrastructure Is Lurking in Victims’ Blind Spots
entry.summary: A new type of malware can worm deep into AI coding systems to steal data and logins—and can flip a “death switch” to destroy files and keep out real users.

entry.title: Nvidia Wants to Own Every Chip Inside AI Data Centers
entry.summary: Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform combines CPUs and GPUs into a single system, reflecting the company’s growing ambition to power every layer of AI infrastructure.

entry.title: ‘It’s a Modern-Day Draft’: Why Stanford Students Walked Out on Sundar Pichai’s Commencement Speech
entry.summary: Last month, more than a hundred Stanford students left their own graduation to protest Google’s military contracts and deals with ICE. Two organizers, Amanda Campos and Eva Jones, tell us why.

entry.title: The Army Is Burning Through Its AI Tokens
entry.summary: Members of the Army received an email informing them that they were rapidly depleting their AI tokens, and needed to limit use.

entry.title: Etsy Is In Its Flop Era, and Sellers Are Fleeing
entry.summary: Once a quirky bastion of amateur vulva jewelry and pet portraits, Etsy is now deluged with mass-produced goods and AI knockoffs. Some customers don’t seem to mind.

entry.title: Headaches for Silicon Valley as China chips away at the US’s lead in the AI race
entry.summary: Google’s AI struggles scream trouble as new Chinese models (again) throw US tech dominance into questionHello, I’m Blake Montgomery, writing to you after a double-header feature of Christopher Nolan’s...

entry.title: How AI may drive union-resistant tech workers to the bargaining table
entry.summary: Tech workers are increasingly unionizing, trading Silicon Valley’s myth of exceptionalism for collective bargaining to contest the corporate deployment of artificial intelligenceFor decades, the techn...

entry.title: Gen Z is living in an intimacy economy, where connection is commodified | Alice Lassman
entry.summary: My generation is struggling to find safety in our human relationships. AI companionship is filling the gap“There are parts of myself I only share with my AI,” Jerome told me. “Your AI?” I asked.He was...

entry.title: Tell us your experiences of living near AI datacentres in the UK
entry.summary: We would like to hear from people living near AI datacentres about their experiences and any concerns they might haveThere are around 450 large data centres across the UK. These warehouses contain com...

entry.title: Man of his word: Pope Leo speeches declared human-authored by Australian AI detection tool
entry.summary: Maps of Hope has been certified by Proudly Human, a company led by former chief scientist, Dr Alan FinkelFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or ...

entry.title: Nine to axe 30 jobs at the Age and SMH due to ‘extreme’ AI disruption
entry.summary: Staff told positions will be cut via voluntary and targeted redundancies as part of ‘evolution’Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news...

entry.title: Election voting advice from AI chatbots ‘inaccurate and unreliable’
entry.summary: Research during Hungary election shows AI recommended parties not running and gave highly volatile answers to identical promptsAI chatbots provide inaccurate, inconsistent and unreliable guidance to v...

entry.title: Not enough water for UK’s datacentre plans, trade body says
entry.summary: Industry says government’s failure to address cooling demands means AI growth plans are ‘fatally flawed’The UK will not have enough water for future datacentres, the water industry has said in stark c...

entry.title: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination
entry.summary: At last, a tech company has found a way to outsource humanity's oldest pastime: using our imaginations.

entry.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
entry.summary: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

entry.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
entry.summary: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

entry.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
entry.summary: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, t...

entry.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
entry.summary: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

entry.title: Neill Blomkamp’s new zombie AI ‘film’ is just slop warmed over
entry.summary: On Monday, District 9 and Gran Turismo director Neill Blomkamp unveiled his latest project: a 13-minute sci-fi short titled Nightborne that's loosely based on Peter Watts' 2014 novel Echoprax...

entry.title: OpenAI says it accidentally hacked Hugging Face with a new AI system
entry.summary:
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. | Bloomberg via Getty Images

OpenAI says its AI models mistakenly breached open-source AI platform Hugging Face during internal testing. In a blog post on Tuesday, OpenA...

entry.title: Substack adds an AI detector to help spot blogs written by no one
entry.summary: Substack will now help users determine whether what they're reading may have been written by AI. A new tool coming to the platform can scan posts, notes, replies, and comments to provide an e...

entry.title: Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book piracy settlement approved by judge
entry.summary: A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, as reported earlier by ...

entry.title: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
entry.summary: Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 ...

entry.title: Advancing next-gen AI with materials science innovation
entry.summary: The conversation about AI often centers on algorithms, computing power, or huge investments in new semiconductor fabrication plants and hyperscale data centers. But beneath each of these advances is a...

entry.title: Why AI Needs a “Genie Coefficient”
entry.summary: Major benchmarks measure what AI can do. None measure whether it does what you mean: the distance between what you ask an AI to do and the unspoken assumptions about how you want the AI to do it. We p...

entry.title: Chinese AI Model Uses Less Muscle for Coding Tasks
entry.summary: Zain Hasan, an AI engineer at Together AI, has taught himself to use AI coding assistants while still keeping an eye on cost. He directs difficult problems to a frontier model, meaning one near the cu...

entry.title: An AI system helped Pakistani judges clear massive backlogs at $38.50 return per dollar invested
entry.summary: A field experiment with 1,559 Pakistani judges found that the AI assistant JudgeGPT boosted case resolution by 6.3 percent. The catch: only judges who got hands-on training saw gains. Without...

entry.title: Claude Cowork learns new skills through screen recordings and voice-over explanations
entry.summary:
Anthropic's Claude Cowork desktop app now lets users record their screen while completing a task, add voice commentary, and have Claude turn the whole thing into a reusable skill.

The article...

entry.title: Google ships three new Gemini Flash models but its frontier 3.5 Pro remains lost in training
entry.summary: Google is shipping three new Flash models in the Gemini series, including the more efficient 3.6 Flash, which uses up to 65 percent fewer tokens, and a cybersecurity model available only to g...

entry.title: New Gemini 3.5 Flash Models Are Faster and Cheaper but Not Smarter
entry.summary: The updated models are intended to be more affordable for enterprises. The new cyber model is designed to orchestrate.

entry.title: OpenAI Urges Enterprises to Use Its Scorecard to Measure Worth of AI
entry.summary: The tool comes amid mounting pressure from low-cost AI providers in China on U.S. AI vendors.

entry.title: Alibaba Qwen 3.8 Max Shows China Closing in on U.S. Models
entry.summary: The low-cost, open-weight model and others from China give enterprises more choices, given the performance claims of some Chinese model providers.

entry.title: Introducing the ChatGPT for small business program
entry.summary: OpenAI launches the ChatGPT for Small Businesses program, helping entrepreneurs build AI skills, automate work, and grow with ChatGPT Work.

entry.title: OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation
entry.summary: OpenAI and Hugging Face share early findings from a security incident during AI model evaluation, highlighting advanced cyber capabilities and lessons for defenders.

entry.title: David Vélez and Robin Vince join the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC
entry.summary: David Vélez and Robin Vince join the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC, bringing global leadership in finance, technology, and governance.

entry.title: The AI Strategy Roadmap: Five drivers of successful AI transformation
entry.summary:
How leaders can assess AI maturity, strengthen the foundations for scale, and build trust into the way transformation happens.

AI has moved quickly from possibility to priority, but many leaders ar...

entry.title: Exploring self-distilled reasoning for supervised fine-tuning with Amazon Nova
entry.summary: When you fine-tune a model using Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), creating high-quality chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning traces for your training data is often impractical and can be prohibitively expens...

entry.title: How to build interactive experiences with canvases
entry.summary: Most developers are now working alongside agents, or are at least familiar with how to do so. Agents help you explore ideas and turn them into action, from planning projects to automating workflows. O...

entry.title: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
entry.summary: We’re introducing new Gemini models, including Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite and 3.5 Flash Cyber.

entry.title: News Summary: July 20, 2026
entry.summary:
• Australian government drafted rules curbing automated AI decisions in agencies
  • Perplexity released WANDR, an open research-agent benchmark across 500 tasks
  • Feyn AI released SQRL, an open text-to-SQL family hitting 70.6% BIRD Dev accuracy
  • Google DeepMind showed video generators can serve as vision world models via GenCeption
entry.content:
• Australian government drafted rules curbing automated AI decisions in agencies
  • Perplexity released WANDR, an open research-agent benchmark across 500 tasks
  • Feyn AI released SQRL, an open text-to-SQL family hitting 70.6% BIRD Dev accuracy
  • Google DeepMind showed video generators can serve as vision world models via GenCeption

entry.title: Alibaba Previews Qwen3.8-Max, a 2.4 Trillion-Parameter Multimodal Model, Days After Moonshot’s Kimi K3 Open-Weight Launch
entry.summary: Continuing our coverage from yesterday, Alibaba previewed Qwen3.8-Max-Preview, a 2.4T-parameter multimodal model at WAIC Shanghai, described as second only to Fable 5. Released two days after Moonshot's Kimi K3 open-weight launch; model card and license not yet published.

entry.title: Alibaba's Qwen takes on Kimi K3 with open-weight Qwen 3.8, says model is "second only to Fable 5"
entry.summary: Continuing our coverage from yesterday, Alibaba unveiled Qwen 3.8, a 2.4T-parameter multimodal open-weight model claimed to trail only Claude Fable 5, with a preview available. The announcement positions it against Moonshot's Kimi K3.

entry.title: Government use of automated AI decision-making to be curbed under new Australian rules
entry.summary: Australia's Albanese government is drafting new national rules to curb automated AI decision-making in government agencies, emphasizing fairness, accuracy and transparency, alongside a digital duty of care push. The plan extends to consumer, workplace and privacy protections.

entry.title: Feyn AI Releases SQRL, a Text-to-SQL Model Family That Inspects the Database Before Writing a Query
entry.summary: Feyn AI released SQRL, an open text-to-SQL model family that inspects databases before querying. Flagship SQRL-35B-A3B hits 70.6% BIRD Dev accuracy, beating Claude Opus 4.6, with three open checkpoints on Hugging Face.

entry.title: Perplexity AI Releases WANDR: An Open Benchmark Evaluating Research Agents That Must Search Wide And Deep
entry.summary: Perplexity released WANDR, an open benchmark for research agents that must collect wide and deep evidence across 500 tasks, complementing its DRACO deep-report benchmark. Targets real knowledge-work evaluation.

entry.title: AI chatbots reading X-rays can be dangerously confident even when they're wrong
entry.summary: Moonshot's Kimi K3 tops Code Arena Frontend ahead of Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol, but scores only ~39% on FrontierMath Tier 4 versus ~90% for OpenAI/Anthropic models. Kimi K3 API date is 2026-07-16, within coverage window.

entry.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
entry.summary: Nonprofit Current AI is building open, culturally inclusive AI infrastructure described as a World Wide Web of AI, with progress across devices and chat. The piece highlights accessibility-focused open AI efforts.

entry.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
entry.summary: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

entry.title: Google Deepmind argues video generators already contain the world models computer vision has been missing
entry.summary: Google Deepmind's GenCeption repurposes a video generator for classic vision tasks such as depth estimation and segmentation, matching state-of-the-art systems with far less training data. Th...

entry.title: Moonshot's Kimi K3 outperforms Fable 5 in frontend code but lags far behind in complex math
entry.summary:
Continuing our coverage from yesterday,

Moonshot's Kimi K3 is the first Chinese model to top the Code Arena: Frontend rankings, beating Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol by a wide margin. But on advanced math, the gap is stark: Kimi K...

entry.title: AI text detectors struggle when language models mimic an author's style
entry.summary: Epoch AI tested three leading AI text detectors (Pangram, GPTZero, and Originality.ai) using style-imitated texts. Up to 18 percent of AI-generated passages went undetected. For scientific wr...

entry.title: 10 Open-Source No-Code AI Platforms for Building LLM Apps, RAG Systems, and AI Agents
entry.summary:
Introduction

Building an LLM application no longer requires wiring orchestration code by hand. A class of open-source platforms now exposes retrieval, agents, and workflows through visual canvases,...

entry.title: Kimi K3 vs DeepSeek V4 Pro vs GLM-5.2: Open Trillion-Scale MoE Models Compared on Benchmarks, License, and Serving Cost
entry.summary: Continuing our coverage from yesterday, Three Chinese labs now hold the top of the open-weight leaderboard. Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3, DeepSeek V4 Pro, and Zhipu AI’s GLM-5.2 are all sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models with milli...

entry.title: Fine-Tuning Qwen3 with LoRA Using NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel: A Complete Single-GPU Google Colab Workflow Tutorial
entry.summary: In this tutorial, we build an end-to-end NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel workflow in Google Colab and use a single GPU to explore the same configuration-driven training architecture that scales to distributed m...

entry.title: huggingface/transformers
entry.summary: 🤗 Transformers: the model-definition framework for state-of-the-art machine learning models in text, vision, audio, and multimodal models, for both inference and training.

entry.title: langflow-ai/langflow
entry.summary: Langflow is a powerful tool for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows.

entry.title: ray-project/ray
entry.summary: Ray is an AI compute engine. Ray consists of a core distributed runtime and a set of AI Libraries for accelerating ML workloads.

entry.title: langfuse/langfuse
entry.summary: 🪢 Open source AI engineering platform: LLM evals, observability, metrics, prompt management, playground, datasets. Integrates with OpenTelemetry, LangChain, OpenAI SDK, LiteLLM, and more. 🍊YC W23

entry.title: agentscope-ai/agentscope
entry.summary: Build and run agents you can see, understand and trust.

entry.title: affaan-m/ECC
entry.summary: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.

entry.title: langchain-ai/langchain
entry.summary: The agent engineering platform.

entry.title: yamadashy/repomix
entry.summary: 📦 Repomix is a powerful tool that packs your entire repository into a single, AI-friendly file. Perfect for when you need to feed your codebase to Large Language Models (LLMs) or other AI tools like C...

entry.title: deepset-ai/haystack
entry.summary: Open-source AI orchestration framework for building context-engineered, production-ready LLM applications. Design modular pipelines and agent workflows with explicit control over retrieval, routing, m...

entry.title: google-gemini/gemini-cli
entry.summary: An open-source AI agent that brings the power of Gemini directly into your terminal.

entry.title: NVIDIA/Megatron-LM
entry.summary: Ongoing research training transformer models at scale

entry.title: qubvel-org/segmentation_models.pytorch
entry.summary: Semantic segmentation models with 500+ pretrained convolutional and transformer-based backbones.

entry.title: huggingface/tokenizers
entry.summary: 💥 Fast State-of-the-Art Tokenizers optimized for Research and Production

entry.title: openvinotoolkit/openvino
entry.summary: OpenVINO™ is an open source toolkit for optimizing and deploying AI inference

entry.title: RunanywhereAI/runanywhere-sdks
entry.summary: Production ready toolkit to run AI locally

entry.title: hao-ai-lab/FastVideo
entry.summary: A unified inference and post-training framework for accelerated video generation.

entry.title: ModelTC/LightX2V
entry.summary: Lightweight Image Video Action Generation Inference Framework

entry.title: open-webui/open-webui
entry.summary: User-friendly AI Interface (Supports Ollama, OpenAI API, ...)

entry.title: infiniflow/ragflow
entry.summary: RAGFlow is a leading open-source Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) engine that fuses cutting-edge RAG with Agent capabilities to create a superior context layer for LLMs

entry.title: n8n-io/n8n
entry.summary: Fair-code workflow automation platform with native AI capabilities. Combine visual building with custom code, self-host or cloud, 400+ integrations.

entry.title: lobehub/lobehub
entry.summary: 🤯 LobeHub is your Chief Agent Operator, organizing your agents into 7×24 operations by hiring, scheduling, and reporting on your entire AI team.

entry.title: thedotmack/claude-mem
entry.summary: claude-mem repo trends; persistent cross-session memory for agents via compression and context injection, supporting Claude Code, Codex, Gemini and others. Tooling, not model release.

entry.title: Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT
entry.summary: AutoGPT repository trending on GitHub; long-running open agent framework for building and using AI agents. No new release in item.

entry.title: Lightning-AI/pytorch-lightning
entry.summary: PyTorch Lightning trending; framework for training/finetuning AI models on 1 to 10000+ GPUs. Established infra.

entry.title: Could AI be conscious?
entry.summary: Duplicate of item 2; The Guardian essay on AI consciousness possibility and ethical planning, referencing Anthropic and Chalmers.

entry.title: News Summary: July 19, 2026
entry.summary: Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.
entry.content: Analysis complete. Top items selected by score.

entry.title: Open-weight models now match frontier cyber performance from just four months ago at a fraction of the cost
entry.summary: The Decoder reports UK AISI findings that open-weight models like GLM-5.2 and DeepSeek V4-Pro now lag closed frontier cyber capabilities by only four to seven months, down from six to ten, with safety mitigations largely ineffective. Open models deliver prior frontier performance at far lower cost.

entry.title: The Pentagon's new AI playbook treats slow adoption as a bigger risk than imperfect alignment
entry.summary: The Decoder covers a US Navy AI strategy that prioritizes rapid adoption of LLMs on warships and an AI war council, framing slow adoption as a greater risk than imperfect alignment. It signals a major military institutional shift toward AI-first operations.

entry.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
entry.summary: TechCrunch covers Moonshot AI's newly released Kimi version this week and the policy debate it sparked among US figures concerned about Chinese AI influence. The article frames the launch as raising geopolitical and competitive questions.

entry.title: Prompt Injection Attacks Are Thwarting AI Hacking Agents
entry.summary: Wired reports on context bombing, a defensive prompt-injection technique that causes malicious AI hacking agents to shut down before causing harm. The method exploits agent vulnerabilities rather than patching them.

entry.title: [AINews] not much happened today
entry.summary: Latent Space's daily roundup notes continued buzz around the Kimi K3 launch from the prior day, Databricks' reported $188B Series M, and OpenRouter acquisition rumors. The author labels it a slow news day.

entry.title: asgeirtj/system_prompts_leaks
entry.summary: A GitHub repo aggregates leaked system prompts from frontier models including Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8, GPT-5.6, Gemini 3.5 Flash, and others. It exposes production prompt engineering from major labs.

entry.title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
entry.summary: Ars Technica examines whether AI deployed in health insurer prior authorization workflows improves efficiency or worsens patient care delays. The piece highlights physician concerns about AI automating denial processes without addressing underlying access problems.

entry.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
entry.summary: Index Ventures co-founder Neil Rimer argues that AI-generated wealth concentration in Silicon Valley will face redistribution pressure, voluntary or forced. The piece is a venture-capital perspective on macro AI economics.

entry.title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
entry.summary: The Verge recounts author Dave Eggers' talk at OpenAI where he criticized ChatGPT for harming educators and 'silencing a generation.' The narrative surfaces cultural backlash against generative AI in writing.

entry.title: How Google’s New Gemini Rates Work and How to Track Your Usage
entry.summary: Wired explains changes to Google Gemini usage quotas and how users can monitor their consumption under the revised rating system. The article is a practical how-to rather than a capability announcement.

entry.title: thedotmack/claude-mem
entry.summary: claude-mem is a trending GitHub project providing persistent cross-session memory for agents including Claude Code and others via compression and context injection.

entry.title: BlinkDL/RWKV-LM
entry.summary: RWKV-LM, an RNN-transformer hybrid at version 7, trends on GitHub as an efficient linear-time architecture.

entry.title: Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT
entry.summary: AutoGPT repository trends on GitHub as an open agentic AI build platform. No new release indicated in the listing.

entry.title: OpenHands/OpenHands
entry.summary: OpenHands, an AI-driven development platform, appears in GitHub trending. No new version detailed.

entry.title: google-gemini/gemini-cli
entry.summary: Google Gemini CLI, an open-source terminal agent for Gemini, trends on GitHub.

entry.title: hao-ai-lab/FastVideo
entry.summary: FastVideo unified video generation inference/post-training framework trends on GitHub.

entry.title: langflow-ai/langflow
entry.summary: Langflow, a tool for building and deploying AI agents and workflows, trends on GitHub.

entry.title: langfuse/langfuse
entry.summary: Langfuse open-source LLM observability and evaluation platform appears in trending.

entry.title: langchain-ai/langchain
entry.summary: LangChain agent engineering platform trends on GitHub; no version news provided.

entry.title: ray-project/ray
entry.summary: Ray distributed AI compute engine listed in GitHub trending with no new release noted.

entry.title: langchain-ai/langgraph
entry.summary: LangGraph agent framework listed in GitHub trending; no release indicated.

entry.title: NVIDIA/Megatron-LM
entry.summary: NVIDIA Megatron-LM large-scale transformer training research repo trends; no new release noted.

entry.title: transformerlab/transformerlab-app
entry.summary: TransformerLab research environment for training/evaluating models trends; no release noted.

entry.title: infiniflow/ragflow
entry.summary: RAGFlow open-source RAG engine with agent capabilities trends on GitHub.

entry.title: tensorflow/tensorflow
entry.summary: TensorFlow core framework listed in GitHub trending; no new release noted.

entry.title: pytorch/pytorch
entry.summary: PyTorch deep-learning library appears in GitHub trending without release annotation.

entry.title: openvinotoolkit/openvino
entry.summary: OpenVINO inference optimization toolkit listed in GitHub trending; no update described.

entry.title: bghira/SimpleTuner
entry.summary: SimpleTuner diffusion model fine-tuning kit trends; no release detailed.

entry.title: n8n-io/n8n
entry.summary: n8n workflow automation platform with native AI capabilities trends on GitHub.

entry.title: vladmandic/sdnext
entry.summary: SD.Next generative image/video WebUI trends on GitHub; no version news.

entry.title: Shubhamsaboo/awesome-llm-apps
entry.summary: Awesome-LLM-apps collection of runnable agent/RAG apps trends on GitHub.

entry.title: tesseract-ocr/tesseract
entry.summary: Tesseract OCR engine listed in GitHub trending; no update described.
